methods.markdown:
## Method

### Corpus and unitization
The corpus comprises 2,500 open-ended exit-survey responses collected January–December 2025 from a single organization [ledger:a1b2c3d4]. Responses were imported from CSV with automatic column-role detection and unitized at the response level (one unit per respondent) [ledger:b2c3d4e5]. Sixty-one responses were flagged as junk and 24 as duplicates; flagged units were excluded from analysis but retained in the bundle [ledger:c3d4e5f6]. Eighteen responses containing personal names were reversibly pseudonymized; the mapping vault is stored outside the project bundle [ledger:d4e5f607].

### Construct and codebook
The construct *Exit theme* (six nominal categories) was drafted by the Director model and subsequently edited and adopted by the research team [ledger:e5f60718]. The codebook specifies inclusion and exclusion criteria, edge-case rules, and worked examples mined from the corpus.

### Gold standard
A simple random sample of n = 20 units was drawn with inclusion probability π = 0.008 per unit, stored at sampling time [ledger:f6071829]. Two blind human coders labeled the sample independently; inter-coder agreement was computed before any machine comparison (κ = .72, α = .71, 95% CI [.48, .89]) [ledger:0718293a]. Disagreements were resolved by adjudication [ledger:18293a4b].

### Instrument
Machine labeling used a single LLM judge (claude-haiku-4-5, snapshot claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, temperature 0, pinned) compiled from the codebook with rationale-before-verdict output [ledger:293a4b5c]. The prompt was tuned against Director-labeled silver data over four iterations (final silver agreement α = .815) [ledger:3a4b5c6d], passed a k = 3 test–retest stability check (α = .88) [ledger:4b5c6d7e], and was frozen at calibration with κ = .79 (95% CI [.61, .94]) against adjudicated gold [ledger:5c6d7e8f].

### Estimation
Reported prevalence and cross-tabulated estimates use design-based supervised learning (DSL): machine labels for all units are combined with gold labels on the π-weighted sample via pseudo-outcomes, yielding estimates unbiased with respect to machine error structure; variances use the sandwich estimator [ledger:6d7e8f90]. Naive plug-in estimates are reported alongside for transparency [ledger:7e8f9001]. Machine accuracy affects precision, not validity.
